36	# Lean Auditor

38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.

42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	For the skeleton we encode a prime divisor by its **generic point** together with
71	a stub witness for the codimension-one + integrality property; this is the
72	standard equivalence (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130: "let `η ∈ Y` be its generic point...
73	the local ring `O_{η,X}` is a discrete valuation ring"). The skeleton's
74	`isCodim1AndIntegral` field is a placeholder `True` that iter-173+ will refine to
75	the genuine `Order.coheight x = 1 ∧ IsIntegral (X.closedSubschemeOfPoint x)`
76	once those notions are extracted; the data field `point` is the substantive one.
77	-/
78	
79	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
80	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
81	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
82	
83	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
84	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
85	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
86	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
87	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
88	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
89	integrality field is needed.
90	
91	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
92	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
93	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
94	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
95	  point : X
96	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
97	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
98	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
99	
100	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
101	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
102	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
103	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
104	
105	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
106	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ

109

276	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
277	
278	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
279	
280	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
281	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
282	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
283	
284	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
285	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
286	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
287	**divisor class group** of `X`.
288	
289	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
290	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
291	
292	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
293	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
294	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf

204	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
205	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
206	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
207	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
208	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
209	  let f : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
210	  let m : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
211	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (m := m) f
212	    (fun i ↦ by
213	      fin_cases i <;> simp [f, m, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
214	        MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X])
215	    (fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> simp [m])
216	    (by
217	      classical
218	      intro p hp
219	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
220	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
221	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
222	        have := hp
223	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
224	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
225	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
226	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
227	        exact this
228	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
229	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
230	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
231	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
232	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
233	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
234	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
235	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
236	        by_contra h
237	        push Not at h
238	        apply hd_ne
239	        ext k
240	        fin_cases k
241	        · simpa using h.1
242	        · simpa using h.2
243	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
244	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
245	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
246	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
247	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
248	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
249	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
250	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
251	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
252	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
253	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))

255	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
256	
257	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
258	private def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
259	  | 0 => 1
260	  | 1 => 0
261	
262	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
263	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
264	
265	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
266	  fin_cases i <;> decide
267	
268	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
269	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
270	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
271	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
272	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
273	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
274	
275	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
276	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
277	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
278	
279	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
280	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
281	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
282	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
283	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
284	
285	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
286	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
287	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
288	
289	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
290	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
291	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
292	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
293	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
294	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
295	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
296	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
297	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
298	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
299	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
300	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
301	
302	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
303	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
304	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
305	    kbar →+*
306	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
307	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
308	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
309	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
310	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
311	
312	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
313	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
314	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
315	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
316	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
317	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
318	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
319	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
320	    (fun _ : Unit =>
321	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
322	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
323	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
324	
325	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
326	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
327	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
328	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
329	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
330	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
331	  · intro r
332	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
333	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
334	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
335	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
336	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
337	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
338	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
339	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
340	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
341	      MvPolynomial.C r
342	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
343	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
344	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
345	  · intro _
346	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
347	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
348	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
349	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
350	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
351	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
352	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
353	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
354	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
355	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
356	      MvPolynomial.X ()
357	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
358	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
359	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
360	
361	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
362	
363	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
364	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
365	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
366	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
367	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
368	
369	Proof structure (iter-172):
370	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
371	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
372	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
373	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
374	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
375	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
376	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
377	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
378	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
379	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
380	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
381	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
382	  classical
383	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
384	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
385	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
386	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
387	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
388	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
389	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
390	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
391	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
392	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
393	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
394	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
395	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
396	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
397	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
398	    apply top_unique
399	    intro p _
400	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
401	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
402	      intro r
403	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
404	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
405	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
406	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
407	      rw [← h]
408	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
409	    · -- add case
410	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
411	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
412	    · -- mul_X case
413	      intro p₁ j hp₁
414	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
415	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
416	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
417	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
418	    v hx dv hxd
419	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
420	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
421	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
422	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
423	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
424	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
425	    apply Subtype.ext
426	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
427	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
428	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
429	    exact key
430	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
431	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
432	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
433	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
434	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
435	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
436	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
437	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
438	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
439	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
440	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
441	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
442	      have h := hai
443	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
444	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
445	      omega
446	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
447	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
448	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
449	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
450	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
451	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
452	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
453	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
454	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
455	    simp only [pow_one]
456	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
457	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
458	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
459	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
460	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
461	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
462	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
463	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
464	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
465	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
466	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
467	      subst heq_i
468	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
469	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
470	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
471	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
472	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
473	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
474	      subst heq_i
475	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
476	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
477	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
478	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
479	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
480	  intro y
481	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
482	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
483	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
484	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
485	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
486	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
487	    rw [htop]; trivial
488	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
489	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
490	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
491	  · -- mem case
492	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
493	    have hgen :=
494	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
495	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
496	    rw [hgen]
497	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
498	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
499	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
500	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
501	    rw [map_pow, hX]
502	  · -- algebraMap case
503	    intro r
504	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
505	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
506	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
507	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
508	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
509	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
510	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
511	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
512	    rw [hr₀]
513	    rfl
514	  · -- add case
515	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
516	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
517	  · -- mul case
518	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
519	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
520	
521	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
522	
523	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
524	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
525	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
526	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
527	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
528	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
529	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
530	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
531	      RingHom.id _ := by
532	  ext x
533	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
534	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
535	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
536	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
537	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
538	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
539	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
540	
541	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
542	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
543	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
544	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
545	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
546	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
547	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
548	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
549	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
550	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
551	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
552	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
553	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
554	
555	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
556	
557	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
558	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
559	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
560	
561	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
562	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
563	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
564	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
565	
566	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
567	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
568	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
569	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
570	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
571	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
572	
573	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
574	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
575	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
576	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
577	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
578	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
579	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
580	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
581	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
582	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
583	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
584	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
585	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
586	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
587	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
588	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
589	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
590	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
591	    exact hi.map _
592	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
593	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
594	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
595	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
596	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
597	
598	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
599	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
600	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
601	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
602	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
603	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
604	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
605	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
606	  Over.homMk
607	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
608	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
609	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
610	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
611	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
612	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
613	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
614	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
615	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
616	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
617	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
618	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
619	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
620	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
621	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
622	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
623	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
624	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
625	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
626	      ext r
627	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
628	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
629	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
630	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
631	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
632	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
633	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
634	
635	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
636	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
637	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
638	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
639	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
640	
641	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
642	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
643	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
644	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
645	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
646	
647	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
648	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
649	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
650	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
651	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
652	
653	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
654	
655	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
656	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
657	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
658	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
659	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
660	
661	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
662	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
663	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
664	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
665	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
666	
667	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
668	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
669	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
670	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
671	
672	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
673	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
674	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
675	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
676	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
677	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
678	
679	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
680	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
681	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
682	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
683	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
684	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
685	
686	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
687	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
688	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
689	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
690	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
691	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
692	
693	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
694	
695	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
696	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
697	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
698	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
699	
700	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
701	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
702	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
703	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
704	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
705	
706	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/

707	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
708	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
709	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
710	
711	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
712	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
713	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
714	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
715	
716	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
717	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
718	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
719	
720	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
721	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
722	element is finitely presented), bridged through
723	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
724	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
725	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
726	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
727	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
728	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
729	      inferInstance)
730	
731	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
732	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
733	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
734	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
735	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
736	
737	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
738	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
739	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
740	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
741	
742	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
743	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
744	  unfold GmRing
745	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
746	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
747	
748	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
749	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
750	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
751	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
752	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
753	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
754	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
755	  infer_instance
756	
757	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
758	
759	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
760	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
761	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
762	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
763	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
764	
765	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
766	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
767	`W = Gm`). -/
768	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
769	
770	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
771	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
772	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
773	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
774	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
775	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
776	
777	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
778	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
779	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
780	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
781	  η[Gm kbar]

783	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
784	
785	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
786	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
787	definition). -/
788	
789	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
790	homogeneous degree-`1` element `f`.
791	
792	Generic in the element `f` so we can apply it to either `(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart
793	input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`) or `X i` (after the
794	`Matrix.cons_val` reduction). A pure rewrite chasing `awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` +
795	the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
796	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
797	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1) :
798	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf Nat.one_pos ≫
799	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
800	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
801	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
802	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
803	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
804	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
805	  rfl
806	
807	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
808	
809	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
810	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
811	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
812	
813	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
814	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
815	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
816	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
817	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
818	
819	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
820	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
821	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
822	
823	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
824	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
825	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
826	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
827	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
828	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
829	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
830	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
831	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
832	
833	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
834	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
835	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
836	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
837	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
838	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
839	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
840	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
841	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
842	        GmRing kbar))
843	
844	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
845	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
846	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
847	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
848	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
849	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
850	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
851	
852	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is `Spec ((Away 𝒜 X_i) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
853	
854	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
855	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
856	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
857	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
858	
859	The case split on `i : Fin 2` is needed because `(projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i`
860	reduces to `Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _`, and `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` is not
861	definitional — it holds via `Matrix.cons_val_zero`/`_one` after `fin_cases`. -/
862	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
863	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
864	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
865	        (TensorProduct kbar
866	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
